signals.title: USD/GBP — G8 Bilateral Theme + Political Turmoil
signals.text: USD/GBP spread at 1.452% marks a dominant G8 theme as GBP/USD breaks below 1.3500. Sterling underperformance is driven by UK political turmoil overshadowing GDP data, while USD strength is reinforced by Fed's Williams and Warsh appointment news signaling a 'higher for longer' stance. Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75% (0bp carry) means price action is currently driven by political risk premia and macro divergence rather than yield advantage. Downside momentum targets 1.3300.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Carry + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.47 remains bid by the +286bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage, yet options markets signal acute caution. 25d RR at -1.69 (strong put skew) indicates heavy institutional hedging against JPY strength despite the carry tailwind. USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation has broken (z=-1.85σ), suggesting the pair is decoupling from Treasury yields as intervention fears or BoJ hike expectations (16 Jun) take precedence. Watch 160.00 for BoJ verbal intervention.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% 18.5%) shows speculators heavily fading the kiwi, while OIS markets price a hawkish bias for the 27 May RBNZ meeting. This positioning extreme vs a hiking bias creates significant short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise. Retail sentiment is 70% long, aligning with the squeeze but contradicting the institutional short. NZD/USD at 0.5891.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 14.6% vs HV30 at 10.5% generates a +4.1% Vol Risk Premium, signaling the options market is pricing in significantly higher volatility than recently realized. This elevated hedging demand coincides with a sharp divergence: COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) vs Retail 82% short. Institutional length is being faded by retail; the high VRP suggests a volatile resolution of this positioning gap. Support at 0.7150.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Complacency + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7847 is supported by a massive +375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage. However, the +5.8% VRP (IV 13.0% vs HV30 7.2%) indicates the market is paying a steep premium for protection, likely against a safe-haven CHF reversal. While VIX at 17.3 (normal volatility) supports carry trades, the extreme VRP suggests institutional desks are not complacent about potential tail risks. Watch for VIX > 25 to trigger carry unwind.
